steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage traces a specific causal chain: water evaporates from the crust → voids form → oil fills those voids. We need the answer that follows directly from this mechanism when sections lose less moisture.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'will develop a thicker crust': crust thickness is never linked to moisture loss in the passage. ✗
  • Choice B — 'will have a lower fat content': less moisture lost → fewer voids → less oil absorbed → lower fat content. This follows directly from the passage's moisture-void-oil chain. ✓
  • Choice C — 'will have a crust that loses permeability less quickly': permeability rate is mentioned as a condition (the crust must remain permeable), not as a consequence of moisture loss. ✗
  • Choice D — 'will have a higher temperature': temperature is not part of the moisture-void-oil pathway the passage describes. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
